text: The new RuleTrack Pediatric Resternotomy Retractor is the first retractor to both lift and spread the surgical site.
This video shows the complex reopening of the sternum of a seven-year-old patient.

The retractor lifts the sternum and spreads the incision as the surgeon dissects tissue off the back of the sternum under direct vision, entering the pleural spaces, facilitating support and dissection.
To begin, the old sternotomy incision is reopened from the skin down to the sternum.
Exposed sternal wires or other closure material are then removed.
The upper linealba is divided below the xiphoid process
and the tissue is dissected.
Finger dissection under the xiphoid and lower sternum is to be avoided as the right ventricle can easily be injured with this maneuver.
While the edges of the linea alba are separated with the assistance of handheld retractors, division of the outer table of the sternum has begun.
Once the lowest part of the sternum is completely divided, the RuralTrac Resternotomy Retractor is set up on the OR table.
The roll track re-sternotomy retractor is safely positioned at the base of the sternum with the rakes on the right and left aspects of the costal margin.

The placement of the retractor provides for ventral encephalic traction in order to facilitate the separation of the sternal edges beginning at the lowest part of the sternum under significantly increased direct or video assisted visualization.
The retractor spreads and lifts the sternum.

The gentle and precise spreading action of the thumb wheel allows for avoidance of sudden wide retraction of the sternal edges to prevent tearing of soft tissues such as the right atrium and ventricle underneath the sternum.
A self retaining Wheatlander style retractor is then placed at the base of the incision to assist in retraction.
